key_insight: The paper examines how stress conditions in 20# steel welded joints promote hydrogen-induced cracking in an acrylonitrile-containing medium, highlighting a coupled environment–stress failure mechanism.
engineering_relevance: The findings are relevant for welded steel structures operating in chemically aggressive service, where joint design and residual stress control can strongly affect cracking risk.
method: Failure analysis of welded joint cracking in service exposure
Assessment of stress-oriented hydrogen-induced crack characteristics
Correlation of fracture/failure features with the acrylonitrile-containing environment
evidence_signal: The abstract indicates a journal article failure-analysis study indexed in OpenAlex, but it does not report quantitative experimental results in the provided text.
watch_item: Because the abstract is brief, the exact test conditions, loading history, and mitigation effectiveness are not specified, so direct design translation should be cautious.
paper_title: Failure Analysis of Stress-Oriented Hydrogen-Induced Cracking of 20# Steel Welded Joints in Acrylonitrile-Containing Medium Environment
